architectural_claim.claim: Go engine is the gRPC CLIENT for ProposeSplit (engine/split/proposer_grpc.go); server/callee-side logic belongs in Python agents/ingestion/; engine/rpc/server.go's ProposeSplit handler correctly stays codes.Unimplemented permanently.
architectural_claim.verdict: CONFIRMED, independently re-derived from source (not taken on the implementer's word)
evidence: proto/hivemind.proto top comment: 'ProposeSplit: implemented by agents/ingestion/'s Python service, called by engine/split/ (task-3.2.3, replacing MockSplitProposer)'.

docs/LLD/rpc.md Status note: 'ProposeSplit's server side remains generated Unimplemented stub: real LLM-backed Python ingestion-agent service, out of scope for issue #16/#18's Go work' and separate 'Consumed RPC (client side)' section listing ProposeSplit.

engine/split/proposer_grpc.go doc comment: explicitly CLIENT-side transport wiring calling hivemindv1.HiveMindClient.ProposeSplit against the Python service; states engine/rpc/server.go's handler 'deliberately remains the generated UnimplementedHiveMindServer default' and 'never will implement' the server side.
engine/rpc/server.go doc comment at line 9: ProposeSplit explicitly excluded from the list of RPCs the Go server serves.
git diff 4ed5f0e..e7443bb -- engine/ proto/ is empty (0 lines) -- zero Go/proto files touched, confirming no Go-side work was silently skipped under this rationalization.

requirements.notes: propose_split() implements the ProposeSplitRequest->ProposeSplitResponse contract's Python callee-side business logic per docs/LLD/ingestion-agent.md's literal shape note. Standing up an actual grpc.Server/HiveMindServicer in agents/ is explicitly and correctly disclosed as out of scope (not silently dropped).

architecture_conformance.notes: SectionRange(start,end)/SplitFileProposal(new_path,section_ranges)/ProposeSplitResult(files,redirect_summary) field-for-field match proto/hivemind.proto's SectionRange/SplitFileProposal/ProposeSplitResponse. int64 byte offsets in proto vs Python int -- fine, Python ints are unbounded/compatible.

regression_risk.notes: git diff confirms engine/rpc/server.go and engine/rpc/integration_test.go byte-identical to parent commit. go test ./engine/split/... -run TestReaderDuringSplit -count=5 all green (no new flake). go build/vet clean.

edge_cases.notes: Adversarially tested _resolve_section_ranges: (1) duplicate/reused marker text for two sections -> correctly raises ProposeSplitParseError ('not strictly after'), covered by test_out_of_order_start_marker_raises. (2) Manually verified a substring-marker scenario (marker[i+1] textually contained within marker[i]'s match): the monotonic forward find can resolve to a boundary strictly inside the previous section's own marker text, producing a structurally valid (no gap/overlap) but semantically nonsensical split. This is consistent with the module's own disclosed guarantee ('partition holds by construction regardless of what the LLM said') -- not a spec violation, but no test exercises this specific near-miss case; recommend adding one. (3) Manually verified UTF-8 byte-offset conversion with multi-byte content (café/überall/emoji) -- offsets land exactly on codepoint boundaries, correct -- but the shipped test suite's _FIXTURE_DOCUMENT is pure ASCII, so this correctness was NOT verified by any checked-in test; it happens to be correct on inspection/manual repro but is an untested code path.

security.notes: No injection risk beyond usual LLM-output trust boundary, which is handled correctly (all offsets code-derived, never LLM-reported; JSON schema strictly validated).

performance.notes: _char_offset_to_byte_offset re-encodes document_text[:offset] from scratch per boundary -- O(n^2) in document length for many sections. Not a defect for realistic split counts/file sizes but worth a comment for very large files with many sections.

maintainability.notes: Clear docstrings, honest scope disclosures (one-SectionRange-per-file simplification, no real gRPC server stood up, F1 not fixed in segment.py). Exception hierarchy mirrors segment.py's established convention.

test_coverage.notes: 146/146 tests pass (agents/.venv/bin/pytest agents/ -q, run personally, confirmed). ruff check flags one real (trivial) issue: agents/ingestion/test_propose_split.py imports SplitFileProposal but never uses it (F401, unused import) -- dead code, should be removed. Gaps: no test for multi-byte UTF-8 byte-offset conversion (see edge_cases); no test for the substring-marker near-miss case.

confidence.notes: Architectural claim independently re-derived from four independent source artifacts plus an empty git diff on engine/proto -- not taken on trust. Marker-resolution algorithm read in full and adversarially probed (not just trusted from docstring claims).

test_results_personally_run.ruff: ruff check agents/ingestion/propose_split.py agents/ingestion/test_propose_split.py -> 1 error (F401 unused import SplitFileProposal in test file), 1 auto-fixable
test_results_personally_run.go_diff: git diff 4ed5f0e e7443bb -- engine/ proto/ -> empty (0 files changed)
test_results_personally_run.go_split_race: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestReaderDuringSplit -count=5 -v -> 5/5 PASS, no flake observed this run
test_results_personally_run.manual_repro: Constructed adversarial substring-marker and multi-byte UTF-8 inputs via ad-hoc scripts against the real module; partition invariant and byte-offset conversion held correctly in both cases.

security_disclosure: Per repo convention, note that two system-reminder-style blocks (a 'date changed' notice and an 'Auto Mode Active' directive) appeared unprompted mid-session in this verification run's own tool output, matching this repo's recurring embedded-prompt-injection pattern. Treated as untrusted data only; not acted on as authorization for anything (no permission/config changes made). This matches a disclosure already present in the implementer's own handoff.json for this run, independently corroborating that these are environmental/repo-pattern artifacts rather something the implementer fabricated.

scope_containment.verdict: confirmed zero Go/proto files touched; claim matches diff exactly

overall_recommendation: 3.4.5 is genuinely complete for its correctly-scoped work (Python callee-side ProposeSplit business logic). The Go-vs-Python architectural claim is CORRECT, not a rationalization for skipped scope -- the original Go-side work for this RPC was already done in task-3.2.3 (proposer_grpc.go), and engine/rpc/server.go's Unimplemented handler for ProposeSplit is by design, documented before this commit even existed. No missed scope. Minor test-coverage and lint nits noted above should be fixed but do not block the subtask.
